_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to consider ethical values and draw evidence-based conclusions from our evaluation of geographical information and data.

_source.w: We are learning to consider ethical values and draw evidence-based conclusions from our evaluation of geographical information and data.
y: Ethics remind us that geographical issues affect real people and communities — our analysis must reflect that.
Evidence-based conclusions are more trustworthy and persuasive than opinions.
Integrating ethical considerations with evidence produces conclusions that are both rigorous and socially responsible.
eg: Drawing a conclusion about water management that considers both environmental sustainability and community needs.
Evaluating the ethics of a development proposal that would affect a culturally significant site.
Using evidence to conclude whether a liveability initiative has been equitable.
Considering whose voices are represented and whose are missing in the data we analysed.
Presenting conclusions that acknowledge uncertainty and the limitations of our evidence.
Discussing the ethical implications of our findings for different stakeholder groups.
